statement.headerDoc:
STEP Agoh-Giuga — Agoh-Giuga conjecture finite verification
  ============================================================

  Agoh-Giuga (Giuga 1950, Agoh 1995):
    n is prime ⟺ Σ_{k=1}^{n-1} k^(n-1) ≡ -1 (mod n)   [for n ≥ 2]

  The ⟹ direction is Fermat's Little Theorem: for prime p,
    each k^(p-1) ≡ 1 (mod p), so the sum is (p-1) · 1 ≡ -1 (mod p).

  The ⟸ direction is OPEN since 1950 (76 years). Necessary conditions for
  a composite counterexample include being both a Giuga number and a
  Carmichael number simultaneously — extremely rare. No such n has been
  found below 10^4890 (Borwe
